title: Short Fiction
description: Short Fiction — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Short Fiction" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Ancient Gods
memes.description: The original Earth colonists of Mars, remembered centuries later as "gods" whose scientific knowledge has been transformed into religious ritual.

memes.label: Anne
memes.description: Norris's wife in "Conditionally Human," whose moral condemnation of her husband's work destroying mutant animals drives the story's central ethical conflict.

memes.label: Anthropos Incorporated
memes.description: A biotechnology corporation in the world of Conditionally Human that pioneered mutant-animal production and neutroid manufacturing using evolvotron technology.

memes.label: Anthropos, Inc.
memes.description: The corporation that manufactures neutroids and other genetically engineered beings in Miller's Federation — the commercial arm of the genetic classification system.

memes.label: Artificial Intelligence
memes.description: The representation of machine intelligence and automated reasoning in Miller's short fiction, from rule-governed robots to systems that blur the boundary between programmed behavior and genuine understanding.

memes.label: Asir
memes.description: A young ritual-thief on a devolved Mars colony, protagonist of "It Takes a Thief," who steals sacred memorized phrases and discovers the planet's dying from lost knowledge.

memes.label: Asir's Scarring
memes.description: The marking of Asir with the rust-rubbed scar — the physical sign of his banishment, transforming his body into a permanent record of the Council's judgment.

memes.label: barkley
memes.description: Barkley was Laskell's superior officer

memes.label: Bermuda Factory
memes.description: The [[neutroid|neutroid]] manufacturing facility where Delmont's negligence occurred — an industrial site that produces engineered substitute children at scale.

memes.label: Big Bottomless
memes.description: The vast region of deep space beyond Earth orbit where the space station floats, referenced by the returning spaceman protagonist of The Hoofer.

memes.label: big-hogey-parker
memes.description: Hogey was returning home to Marie after years in space

memes.label: Big Joe
memes.description: A massive metal guardian robot that protects the vaults beneath Mars in "It Takes a Thief" — a killing machine controlled by a floor-tile puzzle.

memes.label: Big Silence
memes.description: A forty-year communications blackout between the Western Federation and the Asian Proletarian League — the geopolitical status quo that defines the world of "Check and Checkmate" and whose breaching drives the story's central plot.

memes.label: Bioethics in Miller's Fiction
memes.description: The ethical questions raised by genetic engineering and manufactured life in Miller's short fiction — what it means to create beings classified as less than human, and the moral consequences of treating life as product.

memes.label: Blaze of the Winds
memes.description: The ritual-technological concept at the heart of "It Takes a Thief" — simultaneously a rocket engine that can be "kindled" and the central mystery of a cargo-cult religion that has forgotten what the Blaze actually is, guarding its activation as sacred knowledge in temple vaults.
aliases: Kindle the Blaze of the Winds

memes.label: Bleeding-Blade
memes.description: The ritual execution tool of the Mars colony — a knife wielded by the executioner Slubil, whose stroke may deliver death, amputation, or symbolic marking depending on the secret judgment of the Council.

memes.label: Body as Record
memes.description: The recurring motif in Miller's fiction of the body as a physical record of experience — scars, cancers, and markings that tell stories institutions cannot.

memes.label: Bureaucracy in Miller's Fiction
memes.description: The recurring theme of bureaucratic dehumanization across Miller's short fiction — how administrative systems transform moral questions into procedural ones.

memes.label: Capitol
memes.description: The shifting seat of government in "Check and Checkmate," a mobile capitol designed to prevent enemy targeting in an era of perpetual Cold War.

memes.label: Cat-Q-5
memes.description: A genetically engineered feline companion produced for Class-C couples — emotionally safer than neutroids, designed to satisfy the parental instinct without the grief risk of quasi-human substitutes.

memes.label: Character Roster
memes.description: Complete index of all characters appearing in Walter M. Miller Jr.'s short fiction collection, with quality ratings and story appearances.

memes.label: Check and Checkmate
memes.description: A short story by Walter M. Miller Jr. about political intrigue between the Western Federation and the Asian Proletarian League, where President John Smith XVI uses stand-ins to navigate diplomatic crises, collected in Short Fiction.

memes.label: chief-commoner
memes.description: Asir appealed to the Chief Commoner for clemency

memes.label: Clan Structure
memes.description: The clan-based social organization of Mars colony society — kinship groups that structure political power, resource distribution, and social identity.

memes.label: Clans
memes.description: The clan-based social organization of future Earth in "The Ties That Bind," including the tech clans who maintain ancient mechanisms and the Pedaga teaching clan.

memes.label: Class-C Citizen
memes.description: The Federation's lowest genetic classification — citizens with "defective heredity" who are legally barred from reproducing and permitted only engineered pet-substitutes — exposing the eugenic logic at the core of Miller's future society.

memes.label: "Colophon"
memes.description: "Colophon page"

memes.label: Communications Amendment
memes.description: Legislation mentioned in "Check and Checkmate" — part of the Federation's incremental legal restructuring, limiting communication as part of broader social control.

memes.label: Complicity
memes.description: The theme of structural complicity in Miller's fiction — the recognition that living within an unjust system makes one, however unwillingly, a participant in its injustice.

memes.label: Concept Index
memes.description: Alphabetical index of all concepts, themes, and ideas explored across Walter M. Miller Jr.'s short fiction, with quality ratings.

memes.label: Conditionally Human
memes.description: The Federation's system of graded humanity in Miller's stories — where genetic fitness determines reproductive rights and legal personhood exists on a spectrum, rendering "human" a conditional status rather than an inherent one.

memes.label: Conditionally Human
memes.description: Walter M. Miller Jr.'s short story exploring the boundaries of humanity through artificial life forms, neutroids, and the ethical dilemmas of genetic engineering.

memes.label: Congress Investigation (Security Probe)
memes.description: The massive internal security investigation ordered by John Smith XVI after discovering Asian infiltration in his Stand-in ranks — a congressional inquisition that swept up thousands, exposed the regime's paranoia, and revealed the self-devouring logic of authoritarian security apparatuses.

memes.label: Council
memes.description: The governing body of Martian villages and clan assemblies in Miller's short fiction, wielding authority over banishment, law, and communal decisions.

memes.label: Council of Senior Kinsmen
memes.description: The governing body of the Mars colony village in "It Takes a Thief" — a council of elders who administer justice through secret judgment and ritual punishment.

memes.label: Death of a Spaceman
memes.description: A short story by Walter M. Miller Jr. about an elderly spaceman on his deathbed, reflecting on his life and family, collected in Short Fiction.

memes.label: Death of a Spaceman
memes.description: '"Death of a Spaceman" (1954) — An aging spacer confronts his mortality in a story that strips space travel of romance and reveals its human cost.'

memes.label: Delmont
memes.description: A laboratory technician at the Bermuda Factory whose faked neutroid ovum led to the creation of a fertile female neutroid — the central scandal in "Conditionally Human."

memes.label: Delmont Negligency Case
memes.description: The administrative scandal at Anthropos, Inc.'s Bermuda plant — an evolvotron operator's falsified production record that produced an illegally female neutroid — triggering a territory-wide seizure order and the moral crisis at the heart of "Conditionally Human."

memes.label: Doctor Hiram Georges
memes.description: A family physician in "Conditionally Human" whose murder by a woman convinced her neutroid was her stolen baby catalyzes the story's central conflict over mutant animal personhood.

memes.label: Dog-F
memes.description: A genetically engineered canine companion — part of the Federation's graded system of substitute pets for Class-C citizens, offering less emotional entanglement than neutroids.

memes.label: Donny
memes.description: Son of Old Donegal in "Death of a Spaceman," whose relationship with his dying father anchors the story's meditation on legacy and obsolescence.

memes.label: Donny's Death
memes.description: The death of Old Donegal's son Donny — a pre-story event that shapes Donegal's emotional landscape in "Death of a Spaceman."

memes.label: Earth
memes.description: The ancestral homeworld and primary setting for most of Miller's stories, depicted as an overpopulated, bureaucratically managed future society from which characters seek escape through space, memory, or rebellion.

memes.label: Edward
memes.description: A central character in "The Ties That Bind" — invoked in the story's epigraph from the traditional ballad "Edward, Edward."

memes.label: Egg-Multiplier
memes.description: The technology that multiplies chimpanzee ova for neutroid production — part of the industrial apparatus that manufactures quasi-human beings at scale.

memes.label: Eighteenth-Order Virus
memes.description: A viral disease affecting neutroids — treatable in humans but fatal in manufactured substitutes due to the economics of the classification system rather than medical impossibility.

memes.label: Erskine Miler
memes.description: Police Chief of Sherman II who reports on the murder of Doctor Georges in "Conditionally Human," representing the civic authority that exists within but cannot transcend the genetic classification system.

memes.label: Event Index
memes.description: Index of all significant events in Walter M. Miller Jr.'s short fiction collection, from political crises to cultural milestones.

memes.label: Evolvotron
memes.description: The genetic engineering machine used by Anthropos, Inc. to manufacture neutroids — a device that bombards chimpanzee ova with subatomic particles to reshape gene structures into quasi-human forms, embodying Miller's vision of technology as both miracle and moral hazard.

memes.label: evon
memes.description: Ven Klaeden's forces killed Letha before Evon's eyes

memes.label: father-paul
memes.description: Father Paul spoke with Martha about Donegal's condition

memes.label: Federation (Western Federation of Autonomous States)
memes.description: The Western Federation of Autonomous States — Miller's future American superstate — is a bureaucratic authoritarian regime where employment is assigned by aptitude test, reproduction is controlled by genetic classification, and presidents are raised from birth to rule behind golden tragedy masks.

memes.label: Firebirds of the Sun
memes.description: The spacecraft of the Ancient Fathers in Martian mythology — remembered as divine chariots that brought the gods from the heavens.

memes.label: Florida Coast
memes.description: The coastal waters where Lieutenant Laskell surfaces his one-man submarine at the opening of "Way of a Rebel."

memes.label: franklin
memes.description: Franklin sought to impound and test Peony as a suspected deviant neutroid

memes.label: Fried Pie Week
memes.description: A calendar week in the future era of Check and Checkmate, used as a diplomatic deadline between the Western Federation and the Asian Proletarian League.

memes.label: Full Index
memes.description: Complete index of all wiki entries across all types — characters, concepts, places, organizations, events, and works — in Walter M. Miller Jr.'s short fiction collection.

memes.label: Gardens
memes.description: The cultivated garden pathways of future Earth in "The Ties That Bind," where the planet's inhabitants live in quiet harmony with nature in the post-technological era.

memes.label: Secretary Garson
memes.description: A Federation cabinet secretary whose wartime ultimatum in "Way of a Rebel" signals the government's escalation toward total war.

memes.label: Genetic Classification
memes.description: The Federation's system of classifying citizens by genetic fitness — A through C — which determines reproductive rights, social status, and the boundaries of legal personhood.

memes.label: Geoark
memes.description: The governing council of future Earth in "The Ties That Bind," composed of elders who deliberate and decide on behalf of all clans in the post-Exodus era.

memes.label: Murder of Doctor Georges
memes.description: The murder of Doctor Hiram Georges by Sarah Glubbes, whose dying neutroid he had refused to treat — the violent eruption of a grief the genetic classification system was designed to produce but not to acknowledge.

memes.label: Government
memes.description: The federal government system depicted in Miller's short fiction, encompassing the executive, legislative, and military branches in stories of Cold War-era political intrigue.

memes.label: Guilt in Miller's Fiction
memes.description: The pervasive theme of guilt and complicity in Miller's short fiction — characters who recognize their participation in unjust systems but cannot easily extract themselves.

memes.label: Hell Wall
memes.description: A globe-encircling belt of booby-trapped land and ocean separating the Western Federation from the Asian Proletarian League — simultaneously a military barrier, a political tool, and a symbol of the mutual paranoia that defines Miller's divided world.

memes.label: The Hoofer's Return
memes.description: The final journey of the space hoofer in Miller's story — a working-class spacer's last trip home, confronting the human cost of the space lanes.

memes.label: Hüffen
memes.description: A wild flying creature native to Mars, used as a mount by the colonists and invoked as a threat in punishment rituals.

memes.label: "Imprint"
memes.description: "Imprint page"

memes.label: Artificial Life Across Miller's Stories
memes.description: A cross-story analysis of how engineered life — neutroids, mutant animals, and the evolvotron technology — functions as a unifying thematic concern across Miller's short fiction, connecting bioethics, social control, and the boundaries of personhood.

memes.label: Authority and Resistance in Miller's Fiction
memes.description: A cross-story analysis of how Miller portrays the tension between centralized authority and individual resistance — from John Smith XVI's surveillance state to the Martian colonists' ritualized submission — revealing a persistent concern with the moral psychology of obedience.

memes.label: "Martian Society: Decay and Adaptation"
memes.description: "A cross-story analysis of the decaying Martian colony society in Miller's 'It Takes a Thief' — where shipwrecked colonists have lost all technological knowledge, preserved only as ritual, and survive in a pre-industrial culture built on the ruins of their ancestors' spacefaring civilization."

memes.label: It Takes a Thief
memes.description: '"It Takes a Thief" (1952) — Walter M. Miller Jr.''s Mars colony story in which a ritual-thief''s stolen knowledge may be the key to planetary survival.'

memes.label: Ivan Ivanovitch IX
memes.description: The Peoplesfriend and Vicar of the Asian Proletarian League in "Check and Checkmate," John Smith XVI's diplomatic counterpart whose unnerving confidence masks — or reveals — a strategic superiority over the West.
aliases: Vicar of the Asian Proletarian League

memes.label: James Duncan
memes.description: Radio reporter for the Sherman II mobile unit who covers the murder of Doctor Georges in "Conditionally Human."

memes.label: John Smith XVI
memes.description: The sixteenth President of the Western Federation of Autonomous States, protagonist of "Check and Checkmate," who attempts to break forty years of isolation by contacting the Asian Proletarian League.

memes.label: Justice Section
memes.description: A law enforcement and investigative body operating in the occupied zone on Earth, responsible for tracking down escaped personnel and maintaining order among the returning clans.

memes.label: Kinship
memes.description: The role of kinship bonds across Miller's Mars fiction — family as both the deepest human connection and the structure that most tightly constrains individual choice.

memes.label: laskell
memes.description: Laskell served under Captain Barkley's command

memes.label: letha
memes.description: Letha shared living space and friendship with Evon

memes.label: Literary Naturalism
memes.description: Miller's literary naturalism — the refusal to separate moral questions from physical realities, treating bodies, economies, and institutions as material forces that shape human possibility.

memes.label: Manlin Bill
memes.description: The Manlin Bill (Twenty-Sixth Amendment) in "Way of a Rebel" — legislation authorizing military conscription and expanded wartime powers, triggering the story's central moral conflict.

memes.label: Mara
memes.description: Daughter of Senior Kinsman Welkir, a tall and arrogant Mars-colony girl whose ambiguous loyalty to Asir drives the plot of "It Takes a Thief."

memes.label: Mars
memes.description: A colonized frontier world in Miller's fiction, depicted both as a dying planet with an ancient indigenous culture ("It Takes a Thief") and as an industrial alternative to Earth in later stories.

memes.label: Mars Colony
memes.description: The dying human colony on Mars in "It Takes a Thief" — a society that has forgotten its own technological origins and now faces extinction as its atmosphere leaks away.

memes.label: Marswolf
memes.description: A predatory creature of Mars, whose form was used in designing [[big-joe|Big Joe]]'s monstrous head — "with long silver fangs."

memes.label: Martha
memes.description: Old Donegal's devoted wife in "Death of a Spaceman," who has been deliberately sheltered from the truth of her husband's terminal condition.

memes.label: meikl
memes.description: Meikl served as an analyst under Baron ven Klaeden's command

memes.label: Memory and Decay
memes.description: The decay of knowledge across generations in Miller's Mars stories — how technical understanding becomes ritual, then myth, then is lost entirely.

memes.label: Mutant Animals
memes.description: Genetically engineered artificial creatures created by Anthropos Corporation using the evolvotron, sold as pets and companions in Miller's future Earth — occupying a morally ambiguous position between property and personhood.

memes.label: Navy
memes.description: The naval military branch in "Way of a Rebel," the undersea warfare service from which Mitch Laskell deserts after a personal crisis of conscience.

memes.label: Nectar-Cactus
memes.description: A Martian plant producing drinkable nectar, referenced in the children's rhyme Asir recalls while awaiting execution.

memes.label: Neutroid
memes.description: Genetically engineered quasi-human pets in Miller's future — childlike, neuter, and legally disposable — whose existence exposes the Federation's moral contradictions around personhood, commerce, and the human need for something to love.

memes.label: Neutroid Recall
memes.description: The emergency recall of K-99 neutroids triggered by the Delmont negligency case — Norris's district-wide operation to locate, test, and destroy defective manufactured beings.

memes.label: nora
memes.description: Nora is Ken's mother; she supported his decision not to attend the space academy

memes.label: Norris
memes.description: Bio-Administration district inspector and protagonist of "Conditionally Human," caught between his duties destroying unclaimed mutant animals and his wife's moral condemnation of the work.

memes.label: Norris Home
memes.description: The home of Norris and Anne — a domestic space that offers temporary refuge from the moral demands of the pound inspection system.

memes.label: o-reilley
memes.description: Norris investigated O'Reilley's shop during the Bermuda-K-99 survey

memes.label: Occupational Hazard
memes.description: The physical toll of space work in Miller's fiction — radiation exposure, cancer, and the accumulated damage that writes a working life onto the body.

memes.label: Old Donegal
memes.description: Aging former fighter pilot and protagonist of "Death of a Spaceman," facing his final hours in a space hospital with a mix of irreverence, regret, and stubborn dignity.

memes.label: Organization Index
memes.description: Index of all organizations, institutions, and groups appearing in Walter M. Miller Jr.'s short fiction collection.

memes.label: paulson
memes.description: Paulson observed Peony to verify O'Reilley's sanity

memes.label: Peace Effort
memes.description: The recurring and repeatedly failed diplomatic initiatives between the Western Federation and the Asian Proletarian League — each ending in stalemate and tightening the isolation that defines Miller's divided world, until John Smith XVI's televiewphone breach attempts something different.

memes.label: Person Index
memes.description: Index of real people referenced in the wiki — authors, historical figures, and other non-fiction persons.

memes.label: Place Index
memes.description: Index of all locations and settings appearing in Walter M. Miller Jr.'s short fiction collection, from Earth to Mars and beyond.

memes.label: Political Theater
memes.description: The theme of politics as performance in Miller's work — governance reduced to scripted ritual, where the [[presidential-mask|mask]] conceals the absence of genuine authority.

memes.label: District Pound
memes.description: Norris's district pound — the facility where seized and unclaimed neutroids are held in cages, awaiting inspection or destruction.

memes.label: Pound Inspection
memes.description: The emergency recall and inspection of K-99 neutroids in "Conditionally Human" — Norris's frantic effort to locate and test forty potentially defective manufactured beings before they can reproduce.

memes.label: Presidential Mask
memes.description: The golden mask worn by the President of the Federation — hiding the wearer's identity and symbolizing the depersonalization of power in Miller's bureaucratic dystopia.

memes.label: Primary Stand-ins
memes.description: The nine Primary Stand-ins who surround the President — functionaries trained to replace him, each a potential mask-wearer in a system where identity is fungible.

memes.label: Qualifications Amendment
memes.description: The series of constitutional amendments in "Check and Checkmate" that progressively restricted the definition of qualified citizenship — from psychological fitness to education to genetics — creating the Federation's stratified society.

memes.label: Reproduction Rights
memes.description: The Federation's control of reproduction through genetic classification — who may have children and under what conditions, transforming a biological capacity into a state-regulated privilege.

memes.label: Ritual
memes.description: Memorized ritual formulas and chants that serve as currency, knowledge preservation, and sacred tradition in the decaying Martian society of "It Takes a Thief" — the last remnants of a forgotten technology treated as magic.

memes.label: Ritual Economy
memes.description: The economic system of the Mars colony where memorized ritual chants — fragments of scientific and technical knowledge from the ancient colonists — function as currency and wealth.

memes.label: Sanctuary
memes.description: The recurring motif of sanctuary across Miller's fiction — places and relationships that offer temporary protection from systemic cruelty, however fragile and conditional.

memes.label: Sarah Glubbes
memes.description: A Class-C woman whose dying neutroid child and subsequent murder of Doctor Georges expose the human cost of the genetic classification system in "Conditionally Human."

memes.label: Service and Duty
memes.description: The tension between institutional duty and personal conscience in Miller's fiction — what it means to serve a system you have come to recognize as unjust.

memes.label: Sheriff Yates
memes.description: The local sheriff in "Conditionally Human" who assists Norris in enforcing Bio-Administration regulations and seizing unlicensed mutant animals.

memes.label: Sherman II
memes.description: A city in the Western Federation, home to Doctor Georges and the location of his murder in "Conditionally Human."

memes.label: Short Fiction
memes.description: Short fiction as a literary form — compact narratives that achieve their effects through compression, suggestion, and the disciplined deployment of limited space. This collection gathers seven of Walter M. Miller Jr.'s finest stories from 1951–1955.

memes.label: Slubil
memes.description: The village executioner in "It Takes a Thief," a squat man who wields the bleeding-blade with ritual authority — both menacing and bound by law, ultimately sparing the young thief Asir's life.

memes.label: Social Classification
memes.description: The broader theme of social classification across Miller's fiction — how societies sort humans into categories that determine their rights, freedoms, and personhood.

memes.label: Space Travel
memes.description: The technological and cultural context of human spaceflight in Miller's fiction, spanning military combat, commercial transport, and personal aspiration across multiple stories in the collection.

memes.label: Spaceman
memes.description: The figure of the spaceman in Miller's fiction — an aging professional whose body and way of life are being rendered obsolete by the very technology he helped pioneer.

memes.label: Stand-in
memes.description: A tiered system of masked body-doubles and political functionaries in the Western Federation — simultaneously bodyguards, council-members, and advisors — whose anonymity and proximity to power make them indispensable and dangerous in equal measure.

memes.label: Star Exodus
memes.description: The historical diaspora when humanity departed Earth and spread across the galaxy, millennia before the events of The Ties That Bind.

memes.label: "Check and Checkmate"
memes.description: "Check and Checkmate — a short story by Walter M. Miller Jr."

relations.label: Vicar of the Asian Proletarian League

memes.label: "Conditionally Human"
memes.description: "Conditionally Human — a short story by Walter M. Miller Jr."

memes.label: "Death of a Spaceman"
memes.description: "Death of a Spaceman — a short story by Walter M. Miller Jr."

memes.label: "It Takes a Thief"
memes.description: "It Takes a Thief — a short story by Walter M. Miller Jr."

memes.label: "The Hoofer"
memes.description: "The Hoofer — a short story by Walter M. Miller Jr."

memes.label: "The Ties That Bind"
memes.description: "The Ties That Bind — a short story by Walter M. Miller Jr."

memes.label: "Way of a Rebel"
memes.description: "Way of a Rebel — a short story by Walter M. Miller Jr."

memes.label: Technology Index
memes.description: Index of technologies appearing in Miller's fiction — from the evolvotron to the blaze of the winds.

memes.label: Testament
memes.description: The act of bearing witness at the end of life in Miller's fiction — the dying person's testament as a final assertion of meaning against the systems that would erase it.

memes.label: The Hoofer
memes.description: A short story by Walter M. Miller Jr. about a spaceman returning home to Earth after years in deep space, struggling to reconnect with his family, collected in Short Fiction.

memes.label: The Hoofer
memes.description: '"The Hoofer" (1955) — A space hobo''s final journey home, revealing the human cost behind the romance of space travel.'

memes.label: the-peoplesfriend
memes.description: The Peoplesfriend negotiated with President Smith via televiewphone

memes.label: Father Paulson
memes.description: An elderly Catholic priest in "Conditionally Human" who visits Norris's home to investigate a neutroid that can speak, revealing the Church's nuanced position on mutant animals.

memes.label: The Ties That Bind
memes.description: Walter M. Miller Jr.'s story of Earth's reunion with its starfaring descendants, exploring the gulf between those who left and those who stayed behind.

memes.label: Tokra
memes.description: The Chief Commoner of the Mars colony village — the highest civil authority, whose villa [[mara|Mara]] visits to plead for [[asir|Asir]]'s life.

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "Uncopyright page"

memes.label: Ursa
memes.description: A distant star system mentioned in "The Ties That Bind," destination of emigrating ships and home to an alleged human colony in the post-Exodus era.

memes.label: Vaults
memes.description: The sacred underground vaults on Mars in "It Takes a Thief," guarded by priests and the robot Big Joe, containing the technological secrets of the ancients.

memes.label: ven-klaeden
memes.description: Evon witnessed Letha's death at ven Klaeden's command

memes.label: Walter M. Miller Jr.
memes.description: Walter M. Miller Jr. (1923–1996), American science fiction author best known for A Canticle for Leibowitz and a body of acclaimed short fiction including the stories in this collection.

memes.label: Way of a Rebel
memes.description: A short story by Walter M. Miller Jr. about a naval submarine officer who refuses orders to launch missiles during a military crisis, collected in Short Fiction.

memes.label: Way of a Rebel
memes.description: '"Way of a Rebel" (1954) — A Federation military officer must choose between duty and conscience when ordered to suppress a rebellion he secretly sympathizes with.'

memes.label: Welkir
memes.description: A Senior Kinsman of the Mars colony and father of Mara, whose house Asir visits after his banishment in "It Takes a Thief."

memes.label: Work Index
memes.description: Index of all literary works and stories in Walter M. Miller Jr.'s short fiction collection, with quality ratings.

memes.label: Wylo City
memes.description: A city featuring the district wholesale offices of Anthropos, Inc. and multiple pet shops, in the world of Conditionally Human.
